assumptions: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected CircleCI Cloud.
The developer has a GitHub account, an existing repository containing code, and enough GitHub access to authorize or request authorization for the CircleCI GitHub App.

developer_goal: Connect an existing GitHub repository to CircleCI Cloud and obtain the first passing pipeline.

first_success.outcome: CircleCI has created a project linked to the selected GitHub repository, committed or accepted a CircleCI configuration, triggered the first run, and displayed it as passing.
first_success.observable_signal: The CircleCI pipelines page shows the new pipeline in a passing state; expanding its workflow and job reveals successfully completed steps and their output.

route_selection.surface: CircleCI Cloud Quickstart, GitHub App Create Project route
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: CircleCI Cloud Quickstart, GitHub App Create Project route
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: GitLab and Bitbucket Data Center Create Project routes
candidates.reason: These are co-located VCS variants after the first-listed GitHub App route and add provider-specific authorization and configuration behavior.

candidates.name: GitHub OAuth App and Bitbucket Cloud Set Up Project route
candidates.reason: This legacy integration path applies when the web app shows Set up a project rather than Create Project.

candidates.name: Hello world configuration examples
candidates.reason: These examples require an account, connected code, and an already created CircleCI project, while the general Quickstart includes those missing first-mile gates.

required_path.action: Complete CircleCI account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new CircleCI account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected CircleCI candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The CircleCI pipelines page shows the new pipeline in a passing state; expanding its workflow and job reveals successfully completed steps and their output.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A CircleCI Cloud account and organization.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A GitHub account and an existing code repository to build.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S3']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'For the first GitHub project, a GitHub organization administrator or person with repository admin access must install and authorize the CircleCI GitHub App for all or selected repositories.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S3']}

external_gates.description: CircleCI account creation requires email, password, onboarding questions, terms acceptance, privacy acceptance, and reCAPTCHA.
A new developer must create or join a CircleCI organization before project creation.
First-time GitHub App installation and repository authorization can require a GitHub organization administrator or repository admin.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Congratulations, you will soon have a passing pipeline

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Sign up and try CircleCI

sources.title: Create a project in CircleCI

uncertainties.question: Which exact starter configuration does the current GitHub App wizard prepare for a repository without CircleCI config?
uncertainties.impact: The job name and step output shown in the first passing run vary with the wizard's repository-aware selection.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The public current Create Project guide states that CircleCI prepares a config but does not reproduce its dynamic contents; the separate Hello World guide is an optional example, not asserted as the wizard default.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does CircleCI show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
